arguments.task: Open and read official first-party pages relevant to NotebookLM availability through Google Cloud Gemini. Determine whether NotebookLM itself has a public developer API, what authentication methods are documented for any relevant API, whether access is self-serve or gated, whether the API surface is REST or GraphQL, whether an MCP server exists, and collect canonical evidence URLs from the pages actually visited. Prioritize official Google/Google Cloud developer, product, auth, and pricing pages. Also check official NotebookLM product/help pages if linked from Google. Return concise extracted findings with the exact URLs visited and the supporting text.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official pages directly and extract evidence-backed product/API facts for NotebookLM.

raw_result.description: Google’s AI notebook product for summarizing, grounding, and interacting with user-provided sources, with Enterprise availability through Google Cloud.
raw_result.auth_notes: The evidence gathered points to Google/Google Cloud first-party product pages for NotebookLM and Gemini, which commonly use Google account sign-in/OAuth-style access. No first-party developer page was gathered that documents a NotebookLM-specific public API auth scheme, so only oauth2 is supported by current evidence and this remains uncertain for any API usage.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered official page evidence includes a NotebookLM Enterprise page on Google Cloud, indicating enterprise-oriented availability rather than a clearly documented self-serve public developer API for NotebookLM itself.

api_surface.breadth: No official first-party developer documentation was gathered showing a public NotebookLM API surface; the evidence instead points to product and enterprise pages rather than API reference pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party evidence for a NotebookLM MCP server was gathered from the pages visited.

raw_result.blocker: No evidence was gathered of a public, first-party NotebookLM developer API with documented endpoints and authentication sufficient to build a toolkit today.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No NotebookLM-specific API rate-limit documentation was gathered from the official pages visited.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official evidence gathered centered on Gemini and NotebookLM Enterprise marketing/resources rather than a NotebookLM API reference, which is a strong signal that NotebookLM is positioned as a product experience rather than a documented standalone developer API.
raw_result.human_review_reason: A structured field remains unsupported by direct gathered evidence: the exact auth_methods for any buildable NotebookLM API surface cannot be verified because the evidence set does not include a first-party NotebookLM developer/auth reference page. API surface type and verdict are inferred from absence of such documentation in the gathered pages.
